NOTICE OF APPEARANCE FILED BY DEFENDANT KTVU, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW INADEQUATE REMEDY AT LAW, IMMINENT IRREPARABLE HARM. EVIDENCE OF LOST CUSTOMERS IS SPECULATIVE AND CONCLUSORY. VIDEO WAS DISCOVERED IN MID-AUGUST AND RESTRAINING ORDER SOUGHT IN LATE SEPTEMBER. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW A PROBABILITY OF SUCCESS ON ITS CLAIMS, THIRTY DAY STUDY NOT SUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE FALSITY OF STATEMENTS. MANDATORY INJUNCTION IS AN EXTRAORDINARY REMEDY. THIS RULING IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PARTIES SEEKING APPROPRIATE ORDERS AND REMEDIES FROM THE MARIN COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT WHERE THE CLASS ACTION IS PENDING. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Deft Joseph Rotenbberg To Recover Attorney'S Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $12048.08 As Prevailing Deft On "Anti-Slapp" Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp 425.16(C);
Off Calendar May-26-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JOSEPH ROTENBERG, Motion of Defendant Joseph Rotenbberg to Recover Attorney's Fees and Costs in the Amount of $12,048.08 as Prevailing Defendant on "Anti-Slapp" Motion to Strike Pursuant to CCP section 425.16(C). Appearances: Jan A. Yoss, Esq. Younesi & Yoss (213) 229-2400 Appearing thru CourtCall for Plaintiffs and opposing party; Zachary Rothenberg, Esq. of Grodsky & Olecki, (310) 315-3009 Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendants Joseph Rotenberg and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted, $195.00 is subtracted from the amount of $12,048.08, for a total of $11,85.08 awarded. Hourly billing rate reasonable, and work performed reasonable and necessary. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Oct-16-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff's Temporary Restraining Order and order to show cause regarding preliminary injunction. Appearances: Jan A. Yoss, Esq. of Younesi & Yoss LLP (310) 478-5722 appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Jacqueline Scott Corley, Esq. of Kerr & Wagstaffe LLP (415) 371-8500 appearing for Defendants Thomas Howard Clarke, Jr. and Ropers Majeski Kohn & Bentley. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for preliminary injunction denied. Plaintiff fails to show inadequate remedy at law, imminent irreparable harm. Evidence of lost customers is speculative and conclusory. Video was discovered in mid-August and restraining order sought in late September. Plaintiff fails to show a probability of success on its claims, thirty day study not sufficient to demonstrate falsity of statements. Mandatory injunction is an extraordinary remedy. This ruling is without prejudice to parties seeking appropriate orders and remedies from the Marin County Superior Court where the class action is pending. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
